items.definition_en: A storm is a strong disturbance in the weather with wind and rain.
items.example_en: The storm knocked down several trees last night.

items.definition_en: A thunderstorm is a storm with thunder and lightning.
items.example_en: We stayed indoors during the thunderstorm.

items.definition_en: Lightning is a sudden flash of light in the sky during a storm.
items.example_en: Lightning lit up the sky during the night.

items.definition_en: A tornado is a violent spinning wind cloud that touches the ground.
items.example_en: A tornado destroyed several houses in the town.

items.definition_en: A hurricane is a very strong windstorm that forms over the sea.
items.example_en: The hurricane caused heavy damage along the coast.

items.definition_en: A typhoon is a powerful storm that happens in the western Pacific Ocean.
items.example_en: The typhoon brought heavy rain to the islands.

items.definition_en: A gale is a very strong wind.
items.example_en: The ship struggled against the gale.

items.definition_en: A heatwave is a long period of very hot weather.
items.example_en: The heatwave lasted for over a week.

items.definition_en: A cold snap is a short period of very cold weather.
items.example_en: The cold snap surprised everyone in June.

items.definition_en: A gust is a sudden strong wind.
items.example_en: A gust blew my hat off.

items.definition_en: A tropical storm is a strong rainstorm with powerful winds in the tropics.
items.example_en: The tropical storm caused flooding in the city.

items.definition_en: A monsoon is a season of heavy rain in some countries.
items.example_en: The rice fields depend on the monsoon.

items.definition_en: A cyclone is a large storm with strong winds that turn in circles.
items.example_en: A cyclone is expected to reach the coast tomorrow.

items.definition_en: A whirlwind is a fast, spinning wind that lifts things from the ground.
items.example_en: A whirlwind swept up dust from the road.

items.definition_en: A drought is a long period with no rain.
items.example_en: Farmers worry during a drought.

items.definition_en: A flood is when water covers land that is usually dry.
items.example_en: Our school closed after the flood.

items.definition_en: A tidal wave is a very large ocean wave caused by strong winds or an earthquake.
items.example_en: A tidal wave hit the village at midnight.

items.definition_en: A sandstorm is a storm that blows a lot of sand into the air.
items.example_en: The sandstorm made it hard to see outside.

items.definition_en: A dust storm is a strong wind that blows dust over a wide area.
items.example_en: A dust storm can make driving dangerous.

items.definition_en: An avalanche is a large mass of snow and ice that falls down a mountain.
items.example_en: An avalanche blocked the mountain road.
